name: IFS Enterprise Resource Planning API
what_it_does: IFS REST APIs for enterprise resource planning (ERP), enterprise asset management (EAM), and field service management (FSM) platform. Enables AI agents to manage manufacturing production order and work order management for manufacturing operations, handle field service scheduling and work order dispatch for service operations automation, access enterprise asset management data including maintenance schedules and asset lifecycle for asset-intensive industries, retrieve project and contract accounting data for project-based business management, handle supply chain and procurement workflow for manufacturing supply chain automation, access human capital management and payroll data for workforce management, retrieve quality management and non-conformance tracking data for quality operations, manage MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul) data for aviation and defense maintenance programs, handle revenue recognition and financial management for subscription and project billing, and integrate IFS data with IoT sensors, AI platforms, and enterprise technology stacks.
use_cases: Automating production orders from manufacturing agents
Dispatching field technicians from service management agents
Managing asset maintenance from EAM automation agents
Processing MRO work packages from defense maintenance agents
not_for: Simple SMB ERP without complex manufacturing or service management
Non-industrial sectors without asset-intensive or service-intensive operations
Organizations without aviation, defense, or energy sector requirements
best_when: A defense contractor, aviation MRO company, energy company, or industrial manufacturer using IFS wants AI agents to automate production order management, field service dispatch, asset maintenance scheduling, MRO work package management, and project accounting.
avoid_when: SAFETY RISK: IFS manages aviation MRO and defense maintenance data — automated airworthiness directive (AD) compliance tracking and MRO task card closure without certified A&P mechanic authorization creates FAA regulatory violations. Defense MRO automation may be subject to ITAR/EAR export control restrictions. Safety-critical asset maintenance automation must preserve human sign-off requirements per industry maintenance standards (Part 145, AS9100).

auth.notes: IFS uses OAuth 2.0 (OIDC). Developer documentation at IFS Community and docs.ifs.com. Linköping, Sweden HQ. Private (EQT AB acquired 2015). IFS Cloud is the current SaaS platform. IFS Applications is the legacy on-premise product. Strong in aerospace/defense, energy (oil & gas, utilities), manufacturing, and engineering. 10,000+ customers globally. Competes with SAP, Oracle, and Infor for industrial ERP market.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per module (ERP, EAM, FSM) and user count
pricing.notes: Linköping, Sweden. Founded 1983. Private (EQT AB portfolio). 10,000+ customers globally. IFS Cloud (SaaS). Strong in aerospace/defense, oil & gas, utilities, and engineering. Aerospace: Delta Air Lines, Rolls-Royce. Defense: US Air Force, NATO. Energy: BP, Shell. Competes with SAP S/4HANA, Oracle, and Infor for industrial enterprise software.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; cloud plan dependent

known_agent_gotchas: SAFETY RISK: Aviation MRO task card closure automation must require certified A&P mechanic and IA authorization; automated airworthiness sign-off without human certification creates FAA violations and air safety risk
ITAR/EAR defense data — IFS in US defense MRO contains export-controlled technical data; automated data handling must comply with ITAR/EAR access control and data transmission restrictions
IFS Applications vs. IFS Cloud — legacy IFS Applications (on-premise) and IFS Cloud (SaaS) have different API architectures; confirm which version before integration
EQT private equity ownership — EQT portfolio company status means product roadmap and pricing subject to PE dynamics; verify long-term product commitment
Aviation Part 145 compliance — airline MRO operations have FAA Part 145 Repair Station requirements; automated maintenance record creation must maintain regulatory audit trail
AS9100 quality management — aerospace manufacturing in IFS has AS9100 quality management requirements; automated quality record changes require quality manager authorization
agent_readiness.security_notes: Industrial ERP and EAM. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, ITAR. OAuth2/OIDC. US/EU/on-premise. Manufacturing, maintenance, and defense data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Industrial ERP. SOC2, ISO27001, ITAR. OAuth2. US/EU. Manufacturing, defense, and maintenance data.
